article_title: GitHub Pauses Copilot Sign-Ups and Moves Every Plan to Token-Metered Billing as Agentic Workloads Outrun the Subscription Model

content_preview.title: GitHub Pauses Copilot Sign-Ups and Moves Every Plan to Token-Metered Billing as Agentic Workloads Outrun the Subscription Model
content_preview.summary: GitHub froze new Copilot Pro, Pro+, and Student sign-ups on April 20 and announced a June 1 switch to AI Credits, with the Opus 4.7 multiplier rising from 7.5x to 27x.
content_preview.body_excerpt:
## Overview

GitHub spent eight days in late April rewriting the economics of the most widely deployed AI coding assistant on the market. On April 20 the company [paused new sign-ups for Copilot Pro, Pro+, and Student plans](https://github.blog/news-insights/company-news/changes-to-github-copilot-individual-plans/), tightened session and weekly usage limits, and removed Anthropic's Opus models from the Pro tier. A week later, on April 27, it announced that [every Copilot plan will move to usage-...

editor_notes.content_quality: Substantial News piece (1,008 words) with clear structural organization (Overview / What Changed on April 20 / June 1 Transition / Opus 4.7 Multiplier / Developer Reaction / What We Don't Know). Conservative framing despite covering an industry-defining pricing change.

source_verification.https://github.blog/news-insights/compan: source-0.html (datePublished 2026-04-20) verified for: Joe Binder VP of product authorship, 'Agentic workflows have fundamentally changed Copilot's compute demands' quote, 'serve existing customers more effectively' framing, Pro+ has 'more than 5x the limits of Pro', 75% notifications in VS Code/CLI, May 20 refund window, Opus 4.5/4.6 removed from Pro+, Opus 4.7 at 7.5x through April 30.

source-1.html (datePublished 2026-04-27) verified for: June 1, 2026 cutover, AI Credits replacing PRUs, 1 AI Credit = $0.01 USD, Pro $10/month with $10 in monthly AI Credits, Pro+ $39 with $39 credits, Business $19/user/month with $19 credits, Enterprise $39/user/month with $39 credits, Business $30 promotional / Enterprise $70 promotional through August, code completions and Next Edit suggestions remain unlimited, preview bill experience launching early May.
source_verification.https://www.theregister.com/2026/04/20/m: source-2.html (datePublished 2026-04-20) verified for: 'Opus 4.5 and 4.6 models will be removed from Pro+ subscriptions', 'Opus 4.7, launched last week, will be available to Pro+, Teams, and Enterprise customers, in conjunction with a 7.5× premium request multiplier as part of promotional pricing until April 30th', '20 percent to 40 percent more expensive per token than predecessors' (article phrases as '20-40%'), GitHub Community forum discussion engagement.
source_verification.https://www.theregister.com/2026/04/28/m: source-3.html (datePublished 2026-04-28) verified for: Mario Rodriguez chief product officer attribution, 'Today, a quick chat question and a multi-hour autonomous coding session can cost the user the same amount' quote (verbatim), Opus 4.7 multiplier 7.5x → 27x ('Opus 4.7, subject to a 7.5x multiple under request-based billing, will see its multiplier jump to 27 going forward'), GPT-5.4 multiplier 1x → 6x, preview bill experience early May, 'will get less, but pay the same price' framing of community concerns.

editor_notes.factual_accuracy: Every quantitative claim — multipliers, dollar prices, dates, credit allowances, promotional top-ups, refund window — verified verbatim against snapshots. Direct quotes (Binder, Rodriguez) are identical to source text. No fabrications detected.
editor_notes.tone_assessment: Neutral and analytical. The 'Developer Reaction' section accurately characterizes community sentiment without amplifying it; the 'What We Don't Know' section honestly flags open questions about subscriber counts, model rotation timing, and unit-economics outcome.
editor_notes.originality: Original story. No prior Copilot pricing coverage in the archive. Cross-reference to Cursor 3 release article (April 19) is appropriate competitive framing.
recommendations: All sources are official GitHub blog posts and The Register's two related stories. The token-economics math (3.6x faster credit drawdown for Opus 4.7) is the article's own derived calculation from the verified multipliers — a defensible inference rather than a claim attributed to a source.
editor_notes.overall_assessment: APPROVE. High-quality News piece with primary-source GitHub blog verification for all pricing/structure claims and Register corroboration for multiplier numbers and developer reaction. Direct quotes verbatim, no misattributions, conservative framing throughout.
